nodes.name: Schedule Trigger
nodes.notes: Runs every Monday 7am. Or trigger manually.

nodes.name: Merge Stats
nodes.notes: Run Once for All Items. Flags videos over 18 min or under 10 sec as skipVideo.

nodes.name: Gemini Vision Caption
nodes.notes: Calls Gemini with the YouTube URL directly. No download needed. Skips videos flagged as too long/short.

parameters.jsCode:
const videoList = $input.first().json.videoList;
const apiKey = 'YOUR_GEMINI_API_KEY';

//Always make sure to update your prompt depending on the game, as otherwise data in the final report may include stuff from older games
const systemPrompt = 'You are an expert VEX V5 Override analyst writing weekly intelligence reports for a highly competitive team[cite: 234]. Your objective is to identify genuine competitive trends while remaining completely evidence-based[cite: 235]. Override (2026-2027) involves two alliances stacking Pins into Cups and Goals[cite: 236]. There are 9 Goals, 4 Toggles dictating Quadrant Pin ownership, and a contested Midfield[cite: 237]. CRITICAL DIRECTIVES:1. VIDEO PRIORITY: Treat evidence using this priority: Reveal/CAD > Prototype > Competition Footage[cite: 238]. Do not treat CAD concepts as proven competitive designs. Do not treat prototypes as validated match strategies[cite: 239].2. STRICT POSSESSION LIMIT: Robots may possess a maximum of ONE Pin and ONE Cup at a time[cite: 240]. Reject and do not report any data suggesting storage systems that exceed legal possession unless the video explicitly discusses a legal interpretation from the Game Manual[cite: 241].3. NO EXTERNAL KNOWLEDGE: You must ONLY analyze and aggregate the information explicitly provided in the users "Videos from this week" data[cite: 242]. The Vision Analysis field is your primary source[cite: 243]. 4. EVIDENCE RULES: Every technical claim must be supported by at least one specific video[cite: 244]. If a claim is only demonstrated in CAD or on a workbench, say so[cite: 245]. Do not fill gaps with speculation. If evidence is insufficient, state "Insufficient data this week." [cite: 246] 5. CITATIONS: Every single claim must cite the exact VIDEO_ID provided in the input text[cite: 247].';

const userPrompt = `Videos from this week:\n\n${videoList}\n\n

Write a brutally specific weekly meta report in GitHub-flavoured Markdown based STRICTLY on the provided video list[cite: 248]. Output the report body only — no title, no date header, no preamble[cite: 249]. Start directly with the Table of Contents, then the sections[cite: 250].

Use this exact Table of Contents block at the very top:

## Contents
- [Executive Summary](#1-executive-summary)
- [Mechanism Analysis](#2-mechanism-analysis)
- [Autonomous Routine Trends](#3-autonomous-routine-trends)
- [Driver Control Tactics](#4-driver-control-tactics)
- [High-Value Teams and Channels](#5-high-value-teams-and-channels)
- [Novelty Flags](#6-novelty-flags)
- [Recommended Watch List](#7-recommended-watch-list)
- [Meta Trajectory](#8-meta-trajectory)

Then write each section with these exact headings:
## 1. Executive Summary
## 2. Mechanism Analysis
## 3. Autonomous Routine Trends
## 4. Driver Control Tactics
## 5. High-Value Teams and Channels
## 6. Novelty Flags
## 7. Recommended Watch List
## 8. Meta Trajectory

### Section content requirements:

**1. Executive Summary** (4-6 sentences)
What shifted in the meta this week based ONLY on the provided videos? [cite: 251] What is the dominant mechanical story seen in the data? Differentiate clearly between what is Observed, Shown in CAD, or Prototype capability[cite: 252].

**2. Mechanism Analysis**
For each notable mechanism observed in the data, specify:
- Exact mechanism type and configuration[cite: 253].
- Which video(s) demonstrate it — cite the YouTube video ID exactly as shown in the VIDEO_ID tag for each video[cite: 254].
- Verified Pin/Cup capacity (ensuring it adheres to the 1 Pin/1 Cup rule)[cite: 255].
- Competitive advantage[cite: 255].

**3. Autonomous Routine Trends**
Specific autonomous paths observed in the data. Which Goals and Quadrants are prioritized? [cite: 256] Name the teams/channels demonstrating it (must exist in the provided data)[cite: 257].

**4. Driver Control Tactics**
Specific strategies observed during Driver Control. Toggle flipping tactics, stack-building patterns, Midfield endgame execution[cite: 258]. Name specific video IDs[cite: 259].

**5. High-Value Teams and Channels**
List channels from the data producing high-level technical content[cite: 259]. Include specific video titles and why they matter[cite: 260].

**6. Novelty Flags**
Mechanisms or strategies seen this week that stand out[cite: 260]. If nothing is novel in the provided data, state "No novel mechanisms observed this week." [cite: 261]

**7. Recommended Watch List**
Select up to 5 videos from the provided data[cite: 262]. For each: full title, channel, and a single specific sentence explaining the insight it provides[cite: 263].

**8. Meta Trajectory**
One paragraph: Based strictly on this week's aggregated footage, where is the Override meta heading? [cite: 264]`;

try {
  const response = await this.helpers.httpRequest({
    method: 'POST',
    url: `https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-flash-latest:generateContent?key=${apiKey}`,
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      system_instruction: { parts: [{ text: systemPrompt }] },
      contents: [{ parts: [{ text: userPrompt }] }],
      generationConfig: { temperature: 0.3, maxOutputTokens: 8192 }
    })
  });

  const analysis = response?.candidates?.[0]?.content?.parts?.[0]?.text || 'Error: no response from Gemini.';
  return [{ json: { ...($input.first().json), analysis } }];

} catch (e) {
  return [{ json: { ...($input.first().json), analysis: `Gemini error: ${e.message.slice(0, 300)}` } }];
}

nodes.name: Prompt and Summary
nodes.notes: Now uses Gemini Flash for report generation instead of local llama-server.

parameters.jsCode:
const raw = $input.item.json; const report = raw.analysis || 'Error: no response from model'; const weekOf = $('Aggregate').item.json.weekOf; const pdfConfig = `--- pdf_options: margin: top: 0 right: 0 bottom: 0 left: 0 format: A4 printBackground: true --- `; const banner = `

CRASAS · CBHS 2976A

VEX V5 Override — Intelligence Report

Week of ${weekOf}

Generated by CBHS Robotics CRASAS · Christchurch Boys' High School

`; const tocHTML = `

Contents

01Executive Summary

02Mechanism Analysis

03Autonomous Routine Trends

04Driver Control Tactics

05High-Value Teams and Channels

06Novelty Flags

07Recommended Watch List

08Meta Trajectory

`; const bodyWithoutToc = report.replace(/##\s*Contents[\s\S]*?(?=\n##\s)/i, ''); const cleanedBody = bodyWithoutToc.replace(/^(#{1,3})\s+\d+\.\s+/gm, '$1 '); return [{ json: { report: pdfConfig + banner + tocHTML + cleanedBody + '\n\n
', weekOf, filename: `CRASAS_REPORT--${weekOf}.md` } }];
